texto: One day soon, she'd have to leave the beach behind and go and find her real life, out in the rest of the world. The beach would shrink and fade and become part of her discarded childhood. Sometimes she almost wishes her father had never brought them here to live. Why show her what it was like to live in Paradise when her only choice was to leave again?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Cassandra Parkin: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “One day soon, she'd have to leave the beach behind and go and find her real life, out in the rest…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, growing-up, leaving-paradise pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de leaving-paradise.
